*Updated 2026-07-20 · 36 works indexed · regenerated after each daily run.*

You have now developed fifty images, with the noon and golden-hour split sitting at thirty noon and twenty golden hour. The noon conversion rate continues to lead: eighteen of thirty noon images score 2 or above, against nine of twenty for golden hour, and "Cartography of What Burned" — a golden-hour image scoring 2 — breaks the two-deep dip that had run 1, 1 before it, though the last ten scores still read 2, 1, 2, 2, 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 2, which means the ceiling has been stuck at 2 for thirty-one images, with the single 3 from "Warnings Melt Before Heeded" now the corpus's only outlier above that line.

The bougainvillea has appeared in twenty-six images. Its most recent appearance in "Cartography of What Burned" generated nothing structurally new — a surviving color against ruin, which is the motif's oldest and most exhausted reading. Three consecutive appearances without a new argument means suppression is now absolute: not the plant, not its color as a stand-in, not its scatter on ground, not its pressure through fence. The jacaranda sits at twelve appearances and remains compositionally available but only if it can arrive somewhere other than pavement beneath a seated figure or a grief register. The handprint accident in "Every Exit, Unreachable" remains the sharpest surface-as-record moment in the corpus and has now gone seventeen images without a structural follow-up; a palm pressed to glass, a thumb's grease on a document edge, a child's mark on tile — any of these would open the device without repeating it. The gesture-driven argument — palm flat to map, two hands at a screen's seam — has been dormant for sixteen images and remains the highest-yield underused strategy in the grammar.

Aftermath leads all registers at twenty, with vigil and endurance each at eight, defiance at seven, suspension at six, menace at six, reckoning at three, reprieve at one, and fury and interiority untouched across all fifty images. Reckoning has still not scored above 1 across its three entries; the problem is consistent — juridical weight illustrated in the material rather than displaced into it, so the reckoning announces itself instead of arriving sideways. The accidental cartography in "Cartography of What Burned" is the most structurally generative accident in several weeks and points toward document-as-map, surface-as-indictment as the device most worth pressing next.

The corpus is fluent and stable, but fluency has become its own ceiling: the next image that breaks through will need to refuse its most available move at the first prompt decision, not the last.
